name: Custard apple / sugar-apple (Annona squamosa) (food medium)

components.name: D-glucose
components.source: Elhassaneen 2016 (Arab. J. Chem., Sudan Gishta pulp): reducing sugars 17.96 g/100g; glucose:fructose ~50:50 (Chowdhury 2021 PMC7973736; MDPI Foods 2025 14:3413)
components.evidence: Reducing sugars dominate the pulp and are split ~equally between glucose and fructose; half of the measured 17.96 g/100g reducing-sugar fraction assigned to glucose.

components.name: D-fructose
components.evidence: Second half of the 17.96 g/100g reducing-sugar fraction; glucose and fructose are the two dominant free sugars.

components.name: sucrose
components.source: Elhassaneen 2016 (Arab. J. Chem., Sudan Gishta pulp): non-reducing sugar (sucrose) 12.47 g/100g; total sugars 30.43 g/100g
components.evidence: Measured non-reducing sugar as sucrose in ripe pulp. Note: ripe-fruit total-sugar values (18-30 g/100g) vary strongly with cultivar/ripeness; USDA sweetsop total carbohydrate is 23.6 g/100g.

components.name: citric acid
components.source: Elhassaneen 2016 (Arab. J. Chem.): titratable acidity 0.24 g/100g expressed as citric acid; MDPI Foods 2025 14:3413 (citric is the predominant organic acid)
components.evidence: Citric acid is the dominant organic acid of Annona squamosa pulp; total titratable acidity (0.24 g/100g, pH 5.5) is expressed as citric-acid equivalents and is largely citric.

components.name: malic acid
components.evidence: Malic acid is the second organic acid after citric (with minor isocitric acid) but present at low, unquantified levels in the pulp; included as present-but-unquantified.

components.name: total protein
components.source: USDA sugar-apples (sweetsop) raw 2.06 g/100g; Elhassaneen 2016 1.13 g/100g; Chowdhury 2021 2.13 g/100g
components.evidence: Protein is the principal organic-nitrogen source of the pulp; the free/bound amino acids below derive from this fraction.

components.name: L-lysine
components.source: MDPI Foods 2025 14:3413 (amino-acid profile of pulp protein)
components.evidence: Lysine is a protein-derived amino acid of the pulp; per-100g fresh-weight amount not separately quantified (protein ~2 g/100g).

components.name: L-methionine
components.evidence: Sulfur amino acid present in pulp protein; unquantified on fresh-weight basis.

components.name: L-tryptophan
components.evidence: Protein-derived amino acid; unquantified on fresh-weight basis.

components.name: L-ascorbate (vitamin C)
components.source: USDA sweetsop raw 36.3 mg/100g; Elhassaneen 2016 31.22 mg/100mL
components.evidence: Custard apple is a notable vitamin-C source; midpoint of USDA and Sudan measurements.

components.name: thiamine (vitamin B1)
components.evidence: USDA nutrient panel value.

components.name: riboflavin (vitamin B2)
components.evidence: USDA nutrient panel value.

components.name: nicotinate (niacin, vitamin B3)
components.evidence: USDA nutrient panel value.

components.name: pantothenic acid (vitamin B5)
components.evidence: USDA nutrient panel value.

components.name: pyridoxine (vitamin B6)
components.evidence: USDA nutrient panel value; custard apple is a good B6 source.

components.name: folate (vitamin B9)
components.evidence: USDA nutrient panel value (added; missing from current profile).

components.name: beta-carotene
components.source: USDA sweetsop vitamin A ~0-6 ug RAE; MDPI Foods 2025 14:3413
components.evidence: Only trace provitamin-A carotenoid; kept at trace/unquantified level (custard apple has essentially no preformed vitamin A).

components.name: potassium
components.evidence: Dominant mineral cation of the pulp.

components.name: phosphate (phosphorus)
components.evidence: USDA phosphorus value; principal phosphate source.

components.name: calcium
components.evidence: USDA nutrient panel value.

components.name: magnesium
components.evidence: USDA nutrient panel value.

components.name: sodium
components.evidence: USDA nutrient panel value.

components.name: iron
components.evidence: Consolidated single iron entry (replaces compartment-tagged Fe2+/Fe3+ duplicates in current profile).

components.name: zinc
components.evidence: USDA nutrient panel value (range 0.1-0.37 across sources).

components.name: copper
components.evidence: USDA nutrient panel value.

components.name: manganese
components.evidence: Midpoint of reported pulp range; USDA sweetsop panel omits Mn.

components.name: selenium
components.evidence: USDA nutrient panel value (added).

components.name: sulfate
components.evidence: Sulfur source present in pulp; not separately quantified.

components.name: chloride
components.evidence: Present anion; not separately quantified.

components.name: ammonium
components.evidence: Minor free inorganic-nitrogen source; not separately quantified.

components.name: water
components.evidence: Bulk matrix / solvent of the medium.

remove.name: Retinol
remove.reason: Preformed (animal-form) vitamin A; custard apple pulp contains only trace provitamin-A carotenoids, not retinol (USDA vitamin A ~0). FooDB artifact.

remove.name: Nickel
remove.reason: Non-nutritive ultratrace element; not a defined growth nutrient of this fruit.

remove.name: Co2+ (cobalt)
remove.reason: Ultratrace; relevant only as B12 cofactor, and plant pulp has no meaningful cobalt/B12. FooDB trace, not a growth nutrient here.

remove.name: Molybdate
remove.reason: Ultratrace element; not quantified as a nutrient of custard apple. FooDB trace.

remove.name: Fe2+ mitochondria
remove.reason: Compartment-tagged model duplicate of iron; consolidated into a single 'iron' component.

remove.name: Iron (Fe3+)
remove.reason: Redox/compartment duplicate of iron; consolidated into a single 'iron' component.

remove.name: CO2 CO2
remove.reason: Gas-exchange/model metabolite, not a food nutrient contributed by the medium.

remove.name: O2 O2
remove.reason: Gas-exchange/model metabolite, not a food nutrient.

remove.name: H+
remove.reason: Proton/charge-balance model species, not a food nutrient (pH handled separately; pulp pH ~5.5).

remove.name: Barium
remove.reason: Non-nutritive trace contaminant reported in some analyses; not a growth nutrient. Drop.

missing_added: citric acid (dominant organic acid, 0.24 g/100g as titratable acidity)
sources: USDA FoodData Central - Sugar-apples (sweetsop), raw (SR legacy NDB 09071)
USDA FoodData Central - Custard-apple (bullock's-heart), raw (SR legacy NDB 09070)
Elhassaneen et al. 2016, Arabian Journal of Chemistry - Postharvest physicochemical properties of pulp and seed oil from Annona squamosa (Gishta), Sudan
Chowdhury et al. 2021, PMC7973736 - Nutritional, phytochemical and anticancer potential of Annona squamosa fruits
Review of Nutrition, Bioactivities and Health Benefits of Custard Apple (Annona squamosa), MDPI Foods 2025, 14(19):3413 / PMC12523297
notes: Dominant carbon/energy source is free sugars: near-equal glucose and fructose (reducing sugars ~18 g/100g) plus sucrose (~12 g/100g); citric acid is the dominant organic acid (~0.24 g/100g, pulp pH ~5.5) with minor malic/isocitric. Sugar totals vary strongly with ripeness (USDA total carbohydrate 23.6 g/100g vs 30 g/100g in ripe Sudan pulp). Vitamin/mineral panel anchored to USDA sweetsop (A. squamosa); removed model/gas species (CO2,O2,H+), preformed retinol, and ultratrace non-nutrient elements (Ni, Co, Mo, Ba).
